home.tagline: To helping Seniors connect, navigate, and explore with ease.

housing.title: 🏠 Housing Assistance
housing.description: Find affordable senior housing options and emergency shelters.

transportation.title: 🚗 Transportation Support
transportation.description: Learn about senior ride services and mobility aid programs.

hotlines.title: ☎️ Hotlines & Safety
hotlines.description: Contact elder abuse hotlines or mental health support.

hotlinesPage.intro: Trusted hotlines and safety resources for seniors — help is just a call away.

resources.name: Los Angeles County Adult Protective Services (APS)
resources.specialization: Provides 24/7 support for older and dependent adults who may be victims of abuse, neglect, or exploitation. APS investigates reports and offers protective services to ensure the safety and well-being of vulnerable adults.

resources.name: City of Los Angeles Department of Aging – Information & Assistance
resources.specialization: Offers information and referrals for seniors on various services, including transportation, nutrition, caregiver support, and more. This hotline connects older adults and their caregivers to resources that promote independent living.

resources.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program – WISE & Healthy Aging
resources.specialization: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities, addressing concerns related to quality of care, residents' rights, and abuse or neglect. The program works to resolve complaints and improve the quality of life for seniors in care facilities.

housingPage.intro: Here you can find affordable senior housing options and emergency shelters.

resources.name: Los Angeles County Senior Housing Resources
resources.specialization: The Los Angeles County Housing Resource Center website helps provide access to information and tools for finding senior housing and resources. Please continue to check this site for new information. Links to other important websites and agencies and housing locator tools are provided below.

resources.name: Housing Authority of the City of Los Angeles (HACLA)
resources.specialization: HACLA offers a range of programs for low-income, homeless, disabled, children, and older adults. To apply for public housing or get updates on the Section 8 Waiting List, you can contact their main office or visit their website.

resources.name: Affordable Living for the Aging (ALA)
resources.specialization: ALA provides several programs tailored for seniors: Shared Housing: Matches seniors to share housing, converting spare rooms into affordable living spaces. Permanent Supportive Housing: Offers housing with on-site social workers for seniors experiencing chronic homelessness.

resources.name: Los Angeles County Aging & Disabilities Department
resources.specialization: This department provides a list of affordable housing options specific to older adults over 65 years of age, including the Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher Program and utility assistance for those needing help with utility bills.

transportationPage.intro: Explore trusted programs that offer safe and affordable rides for seniors and individuals with limited mobility.

resources.name: County of Los Angeles Transportation and mobile services
resources.specialization: To actively support a more age-friendly Los Angeles County, these transportation and mobility services are dedicated to older and dependent adults.

resources.name: City of Los Angeles Department of Aging Transportation
resources.specialization: The City of Los Angeles offers low-cost and accessible transportation services for seniors, including door-to-door rides, paratransit, and the CityRide program to help with errands, appointments, and more.
